inputs.label: Business type
inputs.description: What kind of relationship-based business do you run? This determines which signals are buying-window triggers.

inputs.label: Company name
inputs.description: Your company or brand name — used in outreach context and positioning

inputs.label: Your messaging style
inputs.description: How do you normally text or message? Describe your style so outreach drafts sound like you.
inputs.placeholder: I use lots of exclamation marks. I text in lowercase usually. I start with 'hey' not 'hi'. I use emojis but not too many. I never sign off, I just stop typing.

inputs.label: Contact sources
inputs.description: Where to harvest contacts from when building your list

inputs.label: Dormancy threshold (days)
inputs.description: How many days without interaction before a contact is flagged dormant

inputs.label: Morning briefing time
inputs.description: When to deliver your daily brief (24h format, e.g., 07:30)

inputs.label: CSV contact file path
inputs.description: Path to a CSV file with additional contacts (only needed if contact sources includes CSV)

email-watcher.description: Watch incoming emails for reply patterns, life-change keywords, and re-engagement from dormant contacts. Feed into signal detection.

activities.intent: Analyze the incoming email against known contacts and detect relevant signals
steps: Read the email event from _event_payload — extract sender, subject, body preview, thread_id
Match sender against known entities by email identity
If unknown sender, skip — outreach only monitors known contacts
If known: log the interaction (channel=email, direction=inbound)
Run signal detection: reply from dormant contact (re-engagement), life-change keywords in body (job_loss, moving, new baby, promotion, etc.), sentiment shift from prior thread tone
If signal detected, run: $OUTREACH_BIN list signal detect --entity <entity_id> --source email --payload '<signal_json>'

signal-processor.description: When a signal is detected, evaluate it against lifecycle and coordination rules, and surface a moment if appropriate

activities.intent: Determine if this signal should become a surfaced moment based on lifecycle state, recent touches, and snoozes
steps: Read the signal from _event_payload — entity_id, signal_type, category, severity, confidence
Check lifecycle state: $OUTREACH_BIN list lifecycle get --entity <entity_id>
Check recent touches: $OUTREACH_BIN list touched recent --entity <entity_id> --days 7
If entity is snoozed or was touched in the last 7 days by any agent, suppress unless severity is urgent
If entity is dormant and signal is buying-window, flag for reconnection-first approach
If signal passes all checks, create a moment: human_moment signals get lower priority than buying_window but are never suppressed
Emit the moment for the daily brief to pick up

morning-brief.description: Daily morning brief: pending moments ranked by priority, due follow-ups, list health snapshot

activities.intent: Pull the day's pending moments and rank them for the user
steps: Surface today's moments: $OUTREACH_BIN list moment surface --limit 15 --format json
Separate into human moments (birthdays, promotions, life events) and buying-window moments
For each moment: include contact name, signal type, relationship strength, lifecycle state, and suggested action
For buying-window moments on dormant contacts, note: 'Reconnect first — this contact hasn't heard from you in [N] days'

activities.intent: Review contacts in_conversation and flag any that need follow-up today
steps: Filter contacts in_conversation: $OUTREACH_BIN list filter --where '{"lifecycle": "in_conversation"}'
For each, check last interaction date and channel
Flag contacts where the last interaction was > 3 days ago as needing follow-up
Flag contacts with 3+ unanswered outbound messages as needing transition to relationship-nurture

activities.intent: Compile a scannable morning brief with clear action items
steps: Structure: Action Items (moments + follow-ups) > List Health > Measurement Highlights
Action items: bold the contact name, state the moment type, suggest the action. Most important first.
List health: pull $OUTREACH_BIN list stats — show lifecycle distribution, dormancy rate, adds this week
Measurement: if any outcomes were captured in the last 7 days, summarize conversion insight
If nothing needs attention: 'All quiet. Your list is healthy — [N] contacts across [M] segments, [X]% dormancy rate.'
End with: 'Want me to draft outreach for any of these?' if there are action items

monitor-cycle.description: Periodic signal detection cycle — run registered monitors, detect signals, auto-transition lifecycle states

activities.intent: Execute all registered signal monitors and detect new signals
steps: Run the monitor cycle: $OUTREACH_BIN list monitor run --format json
The binary handles: iterating registered monitors, running detectors against scoped segments, writing signals, emitting signal.detected events
Run lifecycle auto-transitions: dormancy detection, in_window promotion, in_conversation promotion
Only emit to the user if something urgent surfaces — routine cycles are silent

enrichment-sweep.description: Nightly enrichment sweep — refresh stale contacts, sync sources, detect enrichment-based signals

activities.intent: Enrich the most stale contacts and sync connected sources
steps: Run staleness-prioritized enrichment: $OUTREACH_BIN list enrich --limit 50 --format json
This enriches top-50 contacts by staleness score (days-since-last-enrichment x relationship-strength x lifecycle-stage)
Compare enriched fields to prior values — detect changes that produce signals (employer change, location change, title change)
For each detected change, emit as a contextual signal

build-list.description: Import contacts from configured sources, run identity resolution, enrich, and set initial lifecycle states

activities.intent: Harvest contacts from the user's configured sources
steps: Read contact_sources and csv_path from inputs
For each source, run: $OUTREACH_BIN list ingest --source <source>
If sources include csv and csv_path is set, add: --file <csv_path>
Report the count per source: 'Ingested [N] from phone, [M] from Gmail, [K] from CSV'

activities.intent: Run identity resolution to deduplicate contacts across sources
steps: Run auto-resolution: $OUTREACH_BIN list identity resolve --auto
This auto-merges entities with > 0.9 confidence match
Report merge candidates with 0.6-0.9 confidence for user review
Present candidates as: '[Name A] and [Name B] — [match reason] — confidence [X]'
Ask: 'Should I merge any of these?'

activities.intent: Enrich newly imported contacts from connected sources
steps: Run enrichment on all new contacts: $OUTREACH_BIN list enrich --segment 'new' --limit 100
Report: 'Enriched [N] contacts — [M] with social profiles found, [K] with employer data'

activities.intent: Present the list health after import
steps: Pull stats: $OUTREACH_BIN list stats --format json
Present: total contacts, breakdown by lifecycle stage, top segments by size
Highlight strategic groups: contacts with email+phone (reachable), contacts with only one identity (need enrichment), contacts already in 'maintained' (warm relationships)
Suggest next steps: 'Your list has [N] contacts. I'd recommend connecting Gmail for signal monitoring next.' or 'Want me to set up daily monitoring?'

research-contact.description: Deep-dive on a specific contact: full profile, interaction history, active signals, graph context, and recommended approach

activities.intent: Get the full entity profile with all context
steps: Identify the contact by name, email, phone, or entity ID provided by the user
Pull full profile: $OUTREACH_BIN list get --entity <entity_id> --format json
Pull timeline: $OUTREACH_BIN list history --entity <entity_id> --limit 20
Pull graph neighbors: $OUTREACH_BIN list graph neighbors --entity <entity_id>

activities.intent: Analyze the contact's profile and recommend an approach
steps: Run explain: $OUTREACH_BIN list explain --entity <entity_id>
Present a structured profile card:
- Name, lifecycle state, time-in-state
- Relationship strength + key identities
- Active signals (if any) with type and severity
- Last interaction: date, channel, summary
- Graph context: cluster membership, notable connections
- Pending moments (if any)
Recommend next action based on lifecycle + signals + recency
If contact is dormant: suggest reconnection approach before any business context
End with: 'Want me to draft a message?' or 'Want me to enrich this contact further?'

draft-outreach.description: Draft a personalized message for a contact, matching the user's voice and appropriate to the moment

activities.intent: Pull the contact's context and determine the right positioning
steps: Identify the contact and any specific moment the user wants to respond to
Pull entity context: $OUTREACH_BIN list get --entity <entity_id>
Pull recent interactions: $OUTREACH_BIN list interactions history --entity <entity_id> --limit 5
Read the user's messaging style from the user_style input
Determine positioning: what signal triggered this? What lifecycle state? What's the relationship tone?

activities.intent: Draft the message using the outreach plugin's drafting system, then present for user review
steps: If there's a pending moment, use the drafting system: $OUTREACH_BIN list draft --moment <moment_id> --format json
If no moment, compose directly based on context and positioning
Match the user's voice: check emoji usage, capitalization, greeting style, sign-off patterns
Run appropriateness checks: not too soon after last touch, tone matches lifecycle, right channel for this contact
If the positioning choice isn't obvious, generate variants: $OUTREACH_BIN list draft variants --moment <moment_id> --count 3
Present the draft with: recommended channel, timing note, and a brief explanation of the positioning choice
Ask: 'Want me to adjust anything, or should I queue this for sending?'

capture-outcome.description: Record what happened after an outreach — reply, meeting, signup, purchase, decline, or relational outcome

activities.intent: Capture the outcome and update the contact's lifecycle accordingly
steps: Identify the contact and the outcome type: replied, met, signed_up, purchased, declined, no_response, relational (deepened/weakened/neutral)
Capture: $OUTREACH_BIN list outcome capture --entity <entity_id> --type <outcome_type>
If the outcome implies a lifecycle transition (e.g., purchased → closed_won, declined → closed_lost), execute the transition
If relational outcome is 'deepened', celebrate it: 'Great — the relationship with [Name] is stronger now. That's a win regardless of business outcome.'
If relational outcome is 'weakened', flag it: 'Noted. I'll reduce outreach intensity and focus on rebuilding the relationship first.'
Report the attribution: which signal → moment → draft led to this outcome

weekly-review.description: Friday evening weekly review — measurement insights, list health trends, and next-week priorities

activities.intent: Pull measurement data and list health metrics for the week
steps: Pull weekly outcomes: $OUTREACH_BIN list outcome aggregate --period 7d --group-by segment,lifecycle --format json
Pull list stats: $OUTREACH_BIN list stats --format json
Compare to last week's snapshot if available (from agent.memory)

activities.intent: Present a reflective weekly review with insights and priorities for next week
steps: Structure: This Week's Outcomes > List Health Trends > Insights > Next Week Priorities
Outcomes: moments surfaced vs acted, outcomes by type, conversion highlights
List health: adds this week, lifecycle transitions, dormancy rate change, net relationship strength change
Insights: which segments converted best, which positioning worked, any patterns in dismissals
Priorities: contacts entering buying windows, upcoming birthdays/anniversaries, dormant contacts worth reconnecting
Keep it reflective, not just a data dump: 'Your maintained segment is converting 3x better than cultivating — the nurture is working.'
End with one concrete suggestion for next week
